description: Different events on same day - birthday party vs standup - should NOT match

content_parts.text:
You are comparing calendar events to detect duplicates.

**New Event:**
- Title: Max's 5th Birthday Party
- Start: 2026-03-21T15:00:00
- End: 2026-03-21T18:00:00
- Location: Bounce House Adventure
- Description: Celebrating Max turning 5! Bounce houses, pizza, and cake.

**Existing Events (same date):**

1. Event ID: event-001
   - Title: Daily Standup
   - Start: 2026-03-21T09:00:00
   - End: 2026-03-21T09:15:00
   - Location: Office - Conference Room A
   - Description: Team sync meeting

2. Event ID: event-002
   - Title: Lunch with Client
   - Start: 2026-03-21T12:00:00
   - End: 2026-03-21T13:30:00
   - Location: The Blue Bistro
   - Description: Quarterly business lunch

3. Event ID: event-003
   - Title: Gym Session
   - Start: 2026-03-21T18:30:00
   - End: 2026-03-21T19:30:00
   - Location: 24 Hour Fitness
   - Description: Evening workout

**Question:** Is the new event the same as any of the existing events?

Consider events the same if they refer to the same real-world event, even if:
- Wording is slightly different
- One has more details than the other
- Time changed slightly (updates)

Return the matching Event ID (or null if no match) and brief reasoning.

trace.raw_response_text: {"matched_event_id": null, "reasoning": "The new event (Max's 5th Birthday Party) is scheduled from 15:00 to 18:00 at Bounce House Adventure, which is a birthday party. The existing events on the same date are: - Daily Standup (09:00-09:15, office) - Lunch with Client (12:00-13:30, restaurant) - Gym Session (18:30-19:30, gym). None of the existing events match the time, location, or description of the new event. The activities are distinct (work vs. birthday party)."}
